title: Maschalocorymbus villosus
taxonomy: Maschalocorymbus villosus is a species of fish in the family Cyprinidae. It is native to South America, specifically in the Paraná River basin. This species is considered a type of tetra, which is a common name for small schooling fish. Tetras are generally classified under the order Characiformes, which is a group of fish that are known for their streamlined bodies and large eyes. Further classification of Maschalocorymbus villosus may include species within the family Cyprinidae such as the subfamily Poeciliinae.
